title: Smooth Variational Graph Embeddings for Efficient Neural Architecture Search
abstract: The paper introduces Smooth Variational Graph Embeddings (SVGe), a two-sided variational encoder-decoder GNN for neural architecture search. It improves upon D-VAE by being three times faster, showing improved Bayesian Optimization results, being applicable to various NAS search spaces (ENAS, NAS-Bench-101, NAS-Bench-201), allowing supervised learning of architecture performance prediction, and extrapolating from observed architectures at test time.

method.description: A two-sided variational encoder-decoder GNN for neural architecture search

architecture.encoder: Two GNN modules (forward encoder and backward encoder) to create a latent representation
architecture.decoder: Decodes forward and backward pass separately, generating two graphs sequentially
architecture.output: Union of the two generated graphs

performance: Improved Bayesian Optimization results on ENAS search space
applicability: Can be applied to NAS-Bench-101 and NAS-Bench-201 search spaces
Allows supervised learning of architecture performance prediction
Can extrapolate from observed architectures at test time

contributions: Introduction of a novel graph variational autoencoder, SVGe
